properties.Desc: Umbrella Waterfall is 150 km from Islamabad and it takes 3 hours to get there. Umbrella waterfall is 481 km and 6 hours from Lahore.

The Badshahi Mosque is a Mughal-era congregational mosque in Lahore, capital of the Pakistani province of Punjab, Pakistan. The mosque is widely considered to be one of Lahore's most iconic landmarks.
Bhurban is a small town resort and hill station situated between Murree and Kashmir Road in Punjab province of Pakistan. It is just 11 km away from Murree and 68.8 km away from Islamabad.

This is a magnificent structure in the middle of the Cholistan Desert. It is fortress of monumental proportions, Derawar 40 stunning bastions rise from the desert in a striking square formation. Combined, the fort walls form a circumference of some 1,500
Kanhatti garden was established in 1933 by Major W Whet Burn who was a District Engineer of Shahpore. Kanhat is a natural rock that is found in natural water springs.
Katasraj Mandir  is a Hindu temple complex situated in Katas village near Choa Saidanshah in the Chakwal district of Punjab in Pakistan.

The Khewra Salt Mine is located in Khewra, north of Pind Dadan Khan of Jhelum District, Punjab Region, Pakistan, which rises from the Indo-Gangetic Plain. It is Pakistan's largest and oldest salt mine and the world's second largest source of salt.

The Lahore Fort is a citadel in the city of Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan. The fortress is located at the northern end of walled city Lahore, and spreads over an area greater than 20 hectares. It contains 21 notable monuments, some of which date to the era of

The Wazir Khan Mosque is a 17th-century mosque located in the city of Lahore, capital of the Pakistani province of Punjab. The mosque was commissioned during the reign of the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan as a part of an ensemble of buildings.

Minar-e-Pakistan is a national monument located in Lahore, Pakistan. The tower was built between 1960 and 1968 on the site where the All-India Muslim League passed the Lahore Resolution on 23 March 1940.
Narh Waterfall is a beautiful natural attraction near Kahuta, Pakistan. It is located among the Panjpeer Rocks, cliffs, and peaks, offering stunning views of the surrounding valleys and forests.
Neela Sandh Waterfall and the mini pond is located near Mouri Syedan on the Lehtrar Kotli Syedan Road. It takes 1.5 hours to drive 50 km to Neela Sandh from Islamabad.
The Noor Mahal is a historic real estate owned by the Ministry of Defense under the management of the Army Secretariat in Bahawalpur, Punjab, Pakistan.
Rohtas Fort is a 16th-century fortress located near the city of Jhelum in the Pakistani province of Punjab. The fort is one of the largest and most formidable in the subcontinent.

The Shalimar Gardens, are a Mughal garden complex located in Lahore, Pakistan. The gardens date from the period when the Mughal Empire was at its artistic and aesthetic zenith, and are now one of Pakistan's most popular tourist destinations.
Taxila Museum is located at Taxila, Punjab, Pakistan. The museum is home to a significant and comprehensive collection of Gandharan art dating from the 1st to the 7th centuries CE.

The Thal desert is situated in Punjab, Pakistan. The vast expanse is located between the Jhelum and Sindh rivers near the Pothohar Plateau, with a total length from north to south 190 miles, and a maximum breadth of 70 miles (110 km) and minimum breadth

The Lahore Museum, is a museum located in Lahore, Pakistan. Founded in 1865 at a smaller location and opened in 1894 at its current location on 'The Mall' in Lahore during the British colonial period, Lahore Museum is one of Pakistan's most visited museu
Sheikhupura now headquarter of the district is an ancient town. In A.H. 1015 Emperor Jehangir encamped at Jehangirpura (Present Sheikhupura), which was his hunting place.

Omar Hayat Mahal, also spelt Umer Hayat Mahal, and alternatively known as Gulzar Manzil, is an early 20th-century wooden haveli mansion in Chiniot, Pakistan. The mansion was started in 1923, and was completed by 1935.

Situated on the Multan Road, the monument was actually a gateway to a garden that has now disappeared. It is called Chauburji (the four minarets) because of its four corner minarets, out of which one on the north west corner was actually lost. The fragme

Wagah, an army outpost on Indo-Pak border between Amritsar and Lahore, is an elaborate complex of buildings, roads and barriers on both sides. The daily highlight is the evening,Beating the Retreat ceremony.

Clock Tower of Faisalabad also known as Ghanta Ghar, is a prominent historical monument and a symbol of the city's rich cultural heritage. Built in 1905, the tower stands at a height of 64 meters and is in the centre of the city's bustling markets and ba
The mausoleum dates from 1637, and is located in Shahdara Bagh near city of Lahore, Pakistan, along the banks of the Ravi River.
